blockers.title: Ally members can opt into the talent pipeline
blockers.evidence: Vault Stage 0 says the lane evaluator is the server-side source of truth and that `ally` has talent-pipeline visibility locked out, with women-only pipeline exclusions enforced centrally. The join guard in `convex/members.ts:310-327` and settings guard in `convex/members.ts:373-388` block only `minor` and `restricted_unknown`. Since adult male members evaluate to `ally`, an ally can store `pipeline=true`, creating a consent row that later partner/pipeline code could trust incorrectly.
blockers.required_fix: Treat `ally` as pipeline-ineligible in both join consent handling and `writeConsent`: join should force an explicit false row and audit the refusal; settings should audit, return the error envelope, and write no consent row. Add adult-male regression coverage.

blockers.title: Magic-link limits do not match the vault send policy
blockers.evidence: Vault Stage 0 sets Resend to 3 sends per email per hour and 10 per day, but `convex/lib/rateLimit.ts:20` uses 3 per 15 minutes. The vault send-limit note says Resend free tier hard-caps at 100/day, while `convex/lib/rateLimit.ts:23` allows 200/day and the spec claims that protects the 100/day cap. `convex/auth.ts:73-78` documents that provider failures happen after the verification code is replaced, so hitting Resend's upstream cap before the app cap can still invalidate live links without sending replacements.
blockers.required_fix: Align spec and code to the vault: use 3/hour per email, and keep the app global cap below the active upstream daily cap unless Resend Pro is a documented hard deployment precondition. Add tests for the hourly limit and the global-cap/provider-cap failure mode.

non_blocking_notes: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. `npm test` and `npm run check` could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vitest and Astro attempted to write generated files.
Added member-facing strings in the diff are plain language and add no em dashes.
The committed `.codex/audits/security-hardening.review.json` is a stale earlier DO-NOT-SHIP result and should not be treated as the current Gate 4 record.

spec_compliance.criterion: SEC-1 DOB required at public join boundary
spec_compliance.evidence: `submitJoin` requires `dobAnswer`, validates ISO date/range and 13+ before `createPendingMember`; `createPendingMember` also requires DOB.
`beforeSessionCreation` routes `minor` to `pending_guardian`, `restricted_unknown` to `pending_review`, and issues certificates only for `next === "active"`.

spec_compliance.criterion: SEC-2 rate limiting on magic-link sends
spec_compliance.evidence: The choke point exists, but the constants conflict with the vault source: 3 per 15 minutes instead of 3 per hour, and 200/day does not protect a 100/day upstream cap.
`updateProfile` checks `_storage` metadata, allows only jpeg/png/webp, enforces 5 MB, and returns before patching on invalid photos.
The guard blocks `minor` and `restricted_unknown`, but omits `ally`, which the vault also excludes from talent-pipeline visibility.
`public/_headers` includes the required header families and `astro.config.mjs` rewrites Convex wildcard template origins to `PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL` in build output or strips them when unset.
`@convex-dev/auth` is pinned exactly to `0.0.94` in `package.json` and `package-lock.json`, with an explanatory comment in `convex/auth.ts`.

spec_compliance.criterion: QA-1 unit tests exist in the repo
spec_compliance.evidence: Vitest suites are present for age, lane, lifecycle, profile, rate-limit logic, and Convex integration regressions.

spec_compliance.criterion: QA-2 CI runs type gate and unit tests
spec_compliance.evidence: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `npm run check` and `npm test` before the E2E step.
No new runtime pages or design rewrites were added; runtime UI changes are limited to hardening states and error handling.

summary: Do not ship. The slice closes several issues, but it leaves an authorization/source-of-truth gap for ally pipeline consent and implements send-limit numbers that conflict with the vault and the stated Resend-cap protection.
